sentences.text: Med opmærksomheden rettet mod de nyspartlede huller i min stuevæg
sentences.translation: With my attention fixed on the freshly patched holes in my living room wall

sentences.text: Irettesætter jeg dig med et tonefald, så hårdt, at det ku' bryde is
sentences.translation: I scold you with a tone of voice so harsh it could break ice

sentences.text: Fordi du spilder noget suppe, jeg har varmet op
sentences.translation: Because you're spilling soup I warmed up

sentences.text: Jeg sætter tommeltot og langefinger mellem mine øjne
sentences.translation: I put my thumb and index finger between my eyes

sentences.text: Lukker dem i cirka ni sekunder, mens jeg ekshalerer dybt
sentences.translation: I close them for about nine seconds while I exhale deeply

sentences.text: Lægger hovedet på skrå og ser på dig, før jeg roder rundt i dit grydehår og smiler lidt
sentences.translation: I tilt my head and look at you, before I ruffle your messy hair and smile a little

sentences.text: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i stolen
sentences.translation: You get up and dance a little in the chair

sentences.text: Jeg siger, du ska' sæt' dig ned
sentences.translation: I say you have to sit back down

sentences.text: Du siger, jeg skal sige til mor
sentences.translation: You say I should tell mum

sentences.text: Du aldrig gider sove her igen
sentences.translation: You never want to sleep here again

sentences.text: Så spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud?
sentences.translation: Then you asked me what God looks like

sentences.text: Det er et virkelig godt spørgsmål, hva' tror du?
sentences.translation: That's a really good question, what do you think?

sentences.text: Du svared': »Jeg tror han bor i en landsby i Nordsjælland«
sentences.translation: You answered: "I think he lives in a village in North Zealand"

sentences.text: Jeg parkerer dig i brusenichen med en spand af plastik
sentences.translation: I park you in the shower with a plastic tub

sentences.text: Og legetøj fra BR, som vi har valgt sammen
sentences.translation: And toys from BR that we chose together

sentences.text: Og du synger: »na-na-na-na-na-na«
sentences.translation: And you sing: "na-na-na-na-na-na"

sentences.text: Jeg ka' høre dig, mens jeg rydder op
sentences.translation: I can hear you while I tidy up

sentences.text: Og vi griner, da jeg gi'r dig din pyjamas på
sentences.translation: And we laugh as I put your pyjamas on

sentences.text: Og jeg gnubber mildt, men energisk på din spinkle krop
sentences.translation: And I gently but vigorously rub your slight little body

sentences.text: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i sengen
sentences.translation: You get up and dance a little in the bed

sentences.text: Jeg siger, at du skal læg' dig ned
sentences.translation: I say you have to lie back down

sentences.text: Du siger, jeg skal sige til mor
sentences.translation: You say I should tell mum

sentences.text: Du aldrig gider sove her igen
sentences.translation: You never want to sleep here again

sentences.text: Så spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud
sentences.translation: Then you asked me what God looks like

sentences.text: Det er et virkelig godt spørgsmål, hva' tror du?
sentences.translation: That's a really good question, what do you think?

sentences.text: Du svared': »Jeg tror han bor i en landsby i Nordsjælland«
sentences.translation: You answered: "I think he lives in a village in North Zealand"

grammarPoints.rule: Reflexive verb 'rejse sig' (to stand up / get up)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i stolen.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Rejse sig' = 'to get up'. 'Sig' is the reflexive pronoun. Other reflexive movement verbs: 'sætte sig' (sit down), 'lægge sig' (lie down), 'vende sig' (turn around). These describe movement of the body relative to itself.

grammarPoints.rule: Indirect question with 'hvordan' keeps normal word order
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud?
grammarPoints.explanation: In Danish indirect questions, word order does NOT invert — subject comes before verb. 'Hvordan Gud ser ud' = subject 'Gud' + verb 'ser'. Compare: direct 'Hvordan ser Gud ud?' (inverted) vs indirect 'jeg spørger, hvordan Gud ser ud' (not inverted).

grammarPoints.rule: Separable verb 'se ud' (to look like / to appear)
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Se ud' splits: 'se' stays in verb position, 'ud' goes to the end. Meaning: 'to look like'. 'Hvordan ser han ud?' = 'What does he look like?'. 'Det ser godt ud' = 'That looks good'. One of the most frequent Danish phrasal verbs.
